name: fisheries_management_heat_dome_marrowbridge_st_lt
premise: A Fisheries Management response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving fisheries management heat dome marrowbridge st lt. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as short term vs long term under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Mei Haddad
agents.identity: I am Mei Haddad, accountable for a defensible call in Fisheries Management when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays long term for short term.

agents.name: Kaito Berg
agents.identity: I am Kaito Berg, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Fisheries Management.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so long term isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of short term.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect long term.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Mei Haddad must decide under short term vs long term. What does Mei Haddad do?
